creator: hoek@FreeBSD.org
text:
On Sun, Aug 08, 1999 at 11:20:17AM -0500, chris@calldei.com wrote:
> 
> Now that I have an account to use, I am moving the distfiles location for
> my port to my public_html directory, being the maintainer.

Um, ok, I realize that Nik probably said something about only
committing to the doc repository, but this is ridiculous.

If you're the MAINTAINER of a port, and you're making a trivial change
(such as changing the MASTER_SITE), then just go ahead and do so
unless you've been given some kind of very specific order from say
-core or -asami.

We need all the people closing PRs (either explicitly or preferrably
implicitly) that we can get.  :-)


> -MASTER_SITES=	http://WWW.FreeBSD.ORG/~taoka/distfiles/
> +MASTER_SITES=	http://www.FreeBSD.org/~chris/distfiles/
>  
>  MAINTAINER=	ports@FreeBSD.ORG

If you're the MAINTAINER of the port, I would also suggest changing
the MAINTAINER field.  :)

I'm not sure it's really worthwhile moving MASTER_SITES just for the
sake of moving master sites, but anyways...  You should contact ~taoka
and ~cpiazza and let them know that they don't need to stock these
distfiles anymore.

I think you should make this change (if you really want to move the
MASTER_SITES, that is, and change MAINTAINER if you want to
maintain the ports) and close these two PRs.

If it makes you feel any better, I hereby declare these two
PRs (re: rtfm and fconv) to be officially reviewed and approved by
myself (with appropriate comments about MAINTAINER on fconv, the
usefulness of a gratuitous MASTER_SITE change, and the instruction to
notify ~cpiazza and ~taoka if you still want to change the
MASTER_SITES line).  You can freely blame me when everybody starts
yelling and screaming about how changing the MASTER_SITES broke their
machines.  ;-)


-- 
This is my .signature which gets appended to the end of my messages.
